role_summary: Civil Rights History Project interviewee; the LoC oral history is the primary archival source for this catalog page.
ai_reading: Nathaniel Hawthorne Jones's top semantic neighbor is Charles McLaurin (entry 17, count 124); the broader cluster includes James Oscar Jones (#57), Peggy Jean Connor (#96), Clarence Magee (#21).
biographical_paragraph: Nathaniel Hawthorne Jones's oral history is held at the Library of Congress as entry 92 of the Civil Rights History Project (American Folklife Center accession AFC 2010/039) [src: 1]. The LoC transcript carries a not-auditable audit tier: the catalog cannot independently certify the transcript against primary sources for this entry, and any specific biographical or quoted claims should be confirmed against external archives before citation. Independent biographical sourcing for an extended orientation paragraph is pending; the strongest signal currently available is the embedding pattern named in the AI's reading above.
